quote: Hope has a cost. Hope is not comfortable or easy. Hope requires personal risk. It is not about the right attitude. Hope is not about peace of mind. Hope is action. Hope is doing something. The more futile, the more useless, the more irrelevant and incomprehensible an act of rebellion is, the vaster and more potent hope becomes.  Hope never makes sense. Hope is weak, unorganized and absurd. Hope, which is always nonviolent, exposes in its powerlessness, the lies, fraud and coercion employed by the state. Hope knows that an injustice visited on our neighbor is an injustice visited on all of us. Hope posits that people are drawn to the good by the good. This is the secret of hope's power. Hope demands for others what we demand for ourselves. Hope does not separate us from them. Hope sees in our enemy our own face.

quote: Frobnicate, v.:         To manipulate or adjust, to tweak. Derived from FROBNITZ. Usually abbreviated to FROB. Thus one has the saying to frob a frob. See TWEAK and TWIDDLE. Usage: FROB, TWIDDLE, and TWEAK sometimes connote points along a continuum. FROB connotes aimless manipulation; TWIDDLE connotes gross manipulation, often a coarse search for a proper setting; TWEAK connotes fine
author: tuning. If someone is turning a knob on an oscilloscope, then if he's carefully adjusting it he is probably tweaking it; if he is just turning it but looking at the screen he is probably twiddling it; but if he's just doing it because turning a knob is fun, he's frobbing it.

quote: Laws of Computer Programming (1) Any given program, when running, is obsolete. (2) Any given program costs more and takes longer. (3) If a program is useful, it will have to be changed. (4) If a program is useless, it will have to be documented. (5) Any given program will expand to fill all available memory. (6) The value of a program is proportional to the weight of its output. (7) Program complexity grows until it exceeds the capability of the     programmer who must maintain it. (8) Make it possible for programmers to write programs in English, and you     will find that programmers cannot write in English.

quote: Every Horse has an Infinite Number of Legs (proof by intimidation): Horses have an even number of legs. Behind they have two legs, and in front they have fore
author: legs. This makes six legs, which is certainly an odd number of legs for a horse. But the only number that is both even and odd is infinity. Therefore, horses have an infinite number of legs. Now to show this for the general case, suppose that somewhere, there is a horse that has a finite number of legs. quote: Deep is a word like theory or semantic
author: it implies all sorts of marvelous things. It's one thing to be able to say I've got a theory, quite another to say I've got a semantic theory, but, ah, those who can claim I've got a deep semantic theory, they are truly blessed.

quote: Hacker's Law: The belief that enhanced understanding will necessarily stir a nation to action is one of mankind's oldest illusions.
author: Andrew Hacker, The End of the American Era (1970)

quotes: Critic, n.: A person who boasts himself hard to please because nobody tries to please him.
author: Ambrose Bierce, 'The Devil's Dictionary'

quotes: Honorable, adj.: Afflicted with an impediment in one's reach. In legislative bodies, it is customary to mention all members as honorable; as, 'the honorable gentleman is a scurvy cur.'
author: Ambrose Bierce, 'The Devil's Dictionary'

quotes: A child's education should begin at least 100 years before he is born.
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes

quote: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life. Don't be trapped by dogma
author: which is living with the results of other people's thinking. Don't let the noise of other's opinions drown out your own inner voice. And most important, have the courage to follow your heart and intuition. They somehow already know what you truly want to become. Everything else is secondary.”

quote: The great lesson is that the sacred is in the ordinary, that it is to be found in one's daily life, in one's neighbors, friends and family, in one's backyard.
author: Abraham Maslow

quote: When in doubt, do what the President does
author: guess.

quote: To do easily what is difficult for others is the mark of talent.
author: H. F. Amiel

quote: Be assured that a walk through the ocean of most Souls would scarcely get your Feet wet. Fall not in Love, therefore: it will stick to your face.
author: National Lampoon, Deteriorada

quote: Why is it that our memory is good enough to retain the least triviality that happens to us, and yet not good enough to recollect how often we have told it to the same person?
author: La Rochefoucauld

quote: Why did the opera singer go sailing?
author: They wanted to hit the high Cs.

quote: When the going gets tough, the tough get empirical.
author: Jon Carroll

quotes: Democracy is a form of government that substitutes election by the incompetent many for appointment by the corrupt few.
author: G. B. Shaw

quote: I'm a huge fan of the way you lose control and turn into an enormous green rage monster.
author: The Avengers

quote: Where humor is concerned there are no standards
author: no one can say what is good or bad, although you can be sure that everyone will.

quotes: The road to to success is always under construction.
author: Florian Bruckner

quote: I live in that solitude which is painful in youth, but delicious in the years of maturity.
author: Albert Einstein

quote: In America, any boy may become president and I suppose that's just one of the risks he takes.
author: Adlai Stevenson

quote: If you live for weekends or vacations, your shit is broken
author: Gary Vaynerchuk

quote: Millions of sensible people are too high
author: minded to concede that politics is almost always the choice of the lesser evil. Tweedledum and Tweedledee, they say, I will not vote. Having abstained, they are presented with a President who appoints the people who are going to rummage around in their lives for the next four years. Consider all the people who sat home in a stew in 1968 rather than vote for Hubert Humphrey. They showed Humphrey. Those people who taught Hubert Humphrey a lesson will still be enjoying the Nixon Supreme Court when Tricia and Julie begin to find silver threads among the gold and the black.

quote: Tip the world over on its side and everything loose will land in Los Angeles.
author: Frank Lloyd Wright

quote: Geology shows that fossils are of different ages. Paleontology shows a fossil sequence, the list of species represented changes through time. Taxonomy shows biological relationships among species. Evolution is the explanation that threads it all together. Creationism is the practice of squeeezing one's eyes shut and wailing, Does not!
author: Dr.Pepper@f241.n103.z1.fidonet.org

quote: We are the Knights who say... Ni!
author: Monty Python and the Holy Grail

quote: The real art of conversation is not only to say the right thing at the right place but to leave unsaid the wrong thing at the tempting moment.
author: Dorothy Nevill

quote: It's not who I am underneath, but what I do that defines me
author: Batman Begins

quote: Tonight's forecast: a freeze is coming!
author: Batman & Robin

quote: Don't let anyone ever make you feel like you don't deserve what you want.
author: 10 Things I Hate About You

quote: Some people feel like they don't deserve love. They walk away quietly into empty spaces, trying to close the gaps of the past.
author: Into the Wild

quote: The flush toilet is the basis of Western civilization.
author: Alan Coult

quote: You have bewitched me, body and soul.
author: Pride and Prejudice

quote: I do not fear computers. I fear the lack of them.
author: Isaac Asimov

quote: College football is a game which would be much more interesting if the faculty played instead of the students, and even more interesting if the trustees played. There would be a great increase in broken arms, legs, and necks, and simultaneously an appreciable diminution in the loss to humanity.
author: H. L. Mencken

quote: How do you fix a broken pizza?
author: With tomato paste.

quotes: The cure for boredom is curiosity. There is no cure for curiosity.
author: Dorothy Parker

quote: Imitation is the sincerest form of television.
author: Fred Allen

quotes: Lactomangulation, n.: Manhandling the 'open here' spout on a milk carton so badly that one has to resort to using the 'illegal' side.
author: Rich Hall, 'Sniglets'

quotes: There is some sort of perverse pleasure in knowing that it's basically impossible to send a piece of hate mail through the Internet without its being touched by a gay program. That's kind of funny.
author: Eric Allman

quotes: 'If you go on with this nuclear arms race, all you are going to do is make the rubble bounce.'
author: Winston Churchill

quote: What did the grape do when he got stepped on?
author: He let out a little wine.

quotes: Simplicity does not precede complexity, but follows it.
author: Alan Perlis

quotes: 'There are two ways of disliking poetry; one way is to dislike it, the other is to read Pope.'
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: We've all got both light and darkness inside us. What matters is the part we choose to act on. That's who we really are.
author: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix

quote: Clever girl.
author: Jurassic Park

quotes: Always code as if the person who ends up maintaining your code is a violent psychopath who knows where you live.
author: John Woods

quote: College isn't the place to go for ideas.
author: Hellen Keller

quote: So we follow our wandering paths, and the very darkness acts as our guide and our doubts serve to reassure us.
author: Jean

quote: There's more than one way to skin a cat:         Way number 15
author: Krazy Glue and a toothbrush.

quote: If God had intended us not to masturbate, he would've made our arms shorter.
author: George Carlin

quotes: It's both what you say and the way you say it.
author: There's no point in having great ideas if you don't communicate them effectively.

quote: How do you make the number one disappear?
author: Add the letter G and it's “gone”!

quotes: Some people hope to achieve immortality through their works or their children. I would prefer to achieve it by not dying.
author: Woody Allen
